knowledge_need: Study topic as Engineer-Philosopher. Attempt 1 of up to 3. Give compact Builder-relevant understanding, validation needs, and return plan.

part_digests.name: core meaning
part_digests.meaning: A minimal reusable builder learning rule is the smallest validated rule that can be retained, retrieved, and reused across future build decisions without carrying unnecessary case detail.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Defines X as a bounded learning unit, not a broad philosophy or one-off note.
compact_missing_knowledge: Exact required fields for a rule in this repo
compact_safe_learning_steps: Compare existing accepted atom and retention contracts conceptually
Draft rule shape as trigger, action, boundary, evidence, reuse condition
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Confirm the rule can be retrieved and applied to a later decision with a measurable behavior delta
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X1 to define the candidate object before judging weakness, relevance, or promotion.

part_digests.name: why I am not ideal
part_digests.meaning: The weakness revealed is that the Builder can confuse useful observations with reusable learning, overfit to one case, or retain knowledge that is not decision-active.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Explains why X needs minimality, validation, and reuse boundaries instead of just memory accumulation.
compact_missing_knowledge: Which failure mode is currently dominant
Whether existing retention stores preserve active decision hooks
compact_safe_learning_steps: List common failure modes without changing state
Map each failure to a proposed rule constraint
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Show the rule prevents at least one repeated failure without causing an opposite failure
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X2 to make the rule corrective rather than decorative.

part_digests.name: builder relevance
part_digests.meaning: For self-build or child-agent build, the rule is useful only if it improves future construction choices, acceptance discipline, or route control under bounded context.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Connects X to Builder behavior: better reuse, less drift, smaller validated learning units.
compact_missing_knowledge: Where the rule would be invoked in canonical Builder flow
Whether child-agent build needs the same rule or a stricter variant
compact_safe_learning_steps: Identify one low-risk decision point where the rule would be read
Describe expected decision change before implementation
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Run a before/after decision comparison on a controlled scenario
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X3 to reject rules that are true but not operationally useful.

part_digests.name: validation
part_digests.meaning: Understanding is validated only when the rule is represented, retrieved, applied, and produces an observable decision improvement against a controlled baseline.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Prevents X from being promoted as learning based on explanation quality alone.
compact_missing_knowledge: Canonical validator path for this rule type
compact_safe_learning_steps: Define one bounded smoke scenario and one negative scenario
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Use X4 as the gate before any claim that X is learned.

part_digests.name: promotion decision
part_digests.meaning: Promotion decides whether this is only a case pattern, an atom candidate, or an open gap needing more evidence.
part_digests.role_in_parent_task: Keeps X from entering active knowledge prematurely while still preserving useful draft structure.
compact_missing_knowledge: Promotion criteria for accepted atom retention in the current route
Evidence count needed for reusable status
compact_safe_learning_steps: Classify the draft against explicit criteria
Prefer lower promotion status when evidence is thin
part_digests.compact_validation_needed: Independent check that trigger, boundary, retrieval path, and behavior delta are present
part_digests.return_to_parent_hint: Return X as atom candidate only if validation artifacts exist; otherwise keep it as case pattern or open gap.

dependencies: X2 depends on X1 because weakness must be judged against a clear rule shape
X3 depends on X1 and X2 because relevance requires both definition and failure target
X5 depends on X4 because promotion requires validation evidence
duplicates_or_overlaps: X4 overlaps X5: validation produces the evidence used for promotion
X2 overlaps X3: both concern why the rule matters, but X2 is weakness and X3 is operational use

parent_return_plan.how_to_rebuild_x: Rebuild X as a compact candidate rule with trigger, action, boundary, evidence requirement, retrieval condition, and promotion status; then test whether it changes one future Builder decision under controlled validation.
parent_return_plan.next_small_action: Draft the minimal rule fields and classify the current evidence as case pattern, atom candidate, or open gap without promoting it.
proof_needed: Proof that the rule is retrievable at the right decision point
Proof that applying it changes behavior in a controlled scenario
Proof that it does not promote unvalidated or overbroad learning

promotion_decision.owner_decision_required: only if promotion beyond case pattern is requested
promotion_decision.reason: Batch source answer is material only; default is compact case pattern with part map and parent return plan.
